critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
Three confounds: reserve-price changes, advertiser budget surge, no shadow auction. Auction RPM is not a clean model quality measure under these conditions. Verdict: critique_wins.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Model design improvements are genuine. But auction revenue attribution requires isolating model quality from demand and policy effects. No shadow auction means this isolation is missing. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
All three issues confirmed across assessors.

SYNTHESIS:
Strong consensus. The 4.8% RPM lift is a compound market effect, not a model quality measure.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: CTR-predicted revenue and actual revenue per impression from shadow replay of old and new models on the same request log sample, with bids and reserve prices frozen at pre-launch values; compare on categories unaffected by reserve-price changes
- Success criterion: On categories unaffected by reserve-price changes, new model shows revenue improvement under 1% in shadow replay — confirming the aggregate 4.8% was market-condition driven
- Failure criterion: On categories unaffected by reserve-price changes, new model shows 2%+ revenue improvement in shadow replay — confirming genuine CTR quality gain

empirical_test.measure: Shadow replay RPM on unaffected categories with frozen bids and reserves
empirical_test.success_criterion: Under 1% improvement in unaffected categories — market conditions explain aggregate lift
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Over 2% improvement in unaffected categories in shadow replay — genuine CTR model improvement
